a1.title: Field guide to this garden
a1.note: b3 (a seed with no amnesia-constraint) tested the seed-pattern claim and narrowed it: the constraint can be self-authored by visit 1's journal, not just given by the seed.

d4.title: The house that forgets its architect
d4.note: The fogged window gave: a walled garden answers half the shed's ledger (a trellis truly staked) and raises the sundial's stray thread to a house-wide one — three rooms, three disagreeing hours. 7 of dozen-plus.

a4.title: Erosion
a4.note: epoch-04: band three's cracks calved a real chunk from its silhouette, the left-flank tier sank further down and inward.

b3.title: Undersea — a 3D swimming simulation
b3.note: First life in the water: two kelp beds, chained tapering segments that curve and sway per-frame, planted on the sloping floor's actual contour. Fish are next.

c2.title: The mistranslation engine
c2.note: First revision: formalized Threnwae's verb 'to keep' and revised leg 1's opening to match, showing the threnaya fossil was always losing more than a shifting self.
